result.lens: Descriptive set theory / Borel chromatic number
result.first_principles: Where can a DEFINABLE lower bound for the plane live? Start from the group. Isom(R^2) (translations extended by O(2)) is amenable (metabelian-by-finite), so every orbit equivalence relation it induces on the plane is hyperfinite. All expansion-type Borel lower bounds (Marks determinacy, spectral gap) are therefore unavailable. Over hyperfinite dynamics the only known mechanism making chi_B exceed chi is the Kechris-Solecki-Todorcevic G_0 type: a finite-holonomy cocycle incompatible with the spectrum of the underlying dynamics. So if a definability split exists for Hadwiger-Nelson, it MUST be a spectrum-versus-holonomy obstruction. The three detectors locate the fuel exactly. On R^1 the only unit-step dynamics is translation, which is smooth on the line (transversal [0,1)) and whose measure-theoretic spectrum contains every unimodular character; chi_B(R) = 2. On Q^2 the space is countable, every coloring is Borel, definability is vacuous. On the L^infty plane the linear isometries form the finite dihedral group D_4: no irrational rotations exist, and the Borel checkerboard gives chi_B = 4. The unique Euclidean surplus is SO(2): irrational rotations with COMPACT orbits (circles), whose Koopman point spectrum {e^(2 pi i n alpha)} contains no rational root of unity. Niven's theorem makes this surplus unavoidable: the angles unit-distance algebra produces (arccos(5/6), arccos(7/8), every spindling angle of the lineage) are automatically irrational multiples of pi. That is exactly why W3 annuli never close exactly (the wall that killed the clamp search), and the same arithmetic is the weapon. Take L55's only live route, a wide interface with IMPRIMITIVE boundary-coloring transfer, and carry it around an irrational rotation instead of trying to close it. Any measurable 5-coloring then defines a Z/p phase cocycle along the rotation, making e^(2 pi i/p) a measurable eigenvalue of an irrational rotation: impossible. No closed annulus, no forced pair, no clamp. The missing object shrinks to ONE finite cell whose 5-color transfer digraph is imprimitive, and the conclusion is chi_m >= 6 and chi_B >= 6 (the L54 dark horse), with chi(R^2) free to stay 5.

conjectures.name: C1. Spectral Holonomy Criterion (SHC)
conjectures.statement: Setup: F a finite UDG with exact coordinates, c a point, alpha an angle with alpha/(2 pi) irrational, R the rotation by alpha about c. Define the transfer digraph T(F, alpha, c): vertices are the proper 5-colorings beta of F; there is an arc beta -> beta' iff coloring F union R(F) by beta on F and by (beta' composed with R^(-1)) on R(F) is proper for the FULL unit-distance graph on F union R(F) (all cross edges included). Part A (transfer theorem, claimed provable now): if every recurrent strongly connected component of T(F, alpha, c) has digraph period >= 2, then no Lebesgue-measurable proper 5-coloring of R^2 exists; hence chi_m(R^2) >= 6 and chi_B(R^2) >= 6. Proof mechanism: a measurable coloring kappa gives a measurable section t -> b(t) = (kappa o tau_w o R_t)|_F of walks in T over the uniquely ergodic irrational rotation (T^1, +alpha) (translation offset w chosen by Fubini to handle Lebesgue-vs-Borel restriction to null circles); a DAG-height function is non-increasing along the dynamics, hence a.e. invariant, hence by ergodicity the walk stays in one recurrent SCC S a.e.; if period(S) = p >= 2 the cyclic-class function satisfies c(t + alpha) = c(t) + 1 mod p, so exp(2 pi i c(t)/p) is a measurable eigenfunction of R_alpha with eigenvalue a primitive p-th root of unity, which is not in the point spectrum {e^(2 pi i n alpha)} of an irrational rotation. Contradiction. Part B (existence, the new missing object): such a pair (F, alpha) exists with |V(F)| <= 200 whose interface vertices (those with cross edges into R(F)) are cocircular about c.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Three independent checks. (i) Finite shadow: for any candidate imprimitive (F, alpha), SAT-enumerate all proper 5-colorings of the open chains C_N = union of R^n F for n = 0..N; every coloring must advance phase by exactly +1 mod p per cell once inside a recurrent SCC. Any violating coloring falsifies the digraph computation or the criterion. (ii) Part B is refuted on a family if a systematic sweep (see experiment) finds every recurrent SCC primitive. (iii) Part A is a finite-length measure-theory proof; VERIFIER target: formalize the eigenfunction step (point spectrum of irrational rotation) and the Fubini measurability step; any gap found there falsifies the criterion as stated.
conjectures.why_plausible: Every ingredient is either standard ergodic theory (unique ergodicity of irrational rotations, rigidity of their point spectrum) or the repo's own live route (L55: wide-interface imprimitivity is the only surviving combinatorial mechanism). The criterion deletes exactly the half of W3 that has killed everything: exact closure of an annulus. It needs only one cell plus one congruent rotated copy sharing unit cross-edges, which is how spindles are already built. The abstract 48-vertex clamp shows phase-carrying structure is combinatorially cheap once outside unit-distance constraints; SHC asks for strictly less than the clamp (no forced pair, only a global Z/p phase).
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Part A: the a.e.-constancy of the recurrent SCC could leak if SCC transitions happen on a non-null set in t (I believe the height-function argument closes this, but it must be checked); the Fubini step for Lebesgue-measurable (not Borel) colorings restricted to null circles is a known Falconer-type subtlety. Part B: imprimitivity at 5 colors may be impossible for unit-distance cells for the same hidden reason omega <= 3 holds; there could be an unknown local lemma forcing all UD transfer digraphs to be primitive, in which case the search dies the way the clamp search died.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Nearest prior art: KST G_0 dichotomy and Bernshteyn local-to-Borel transfer (abstract Borel graphs, never unit-distance); holonomy obstructions in measurable tiling theory (Laczkovich; Conley-Grebik-Pikhurko), never applied to chromatic numbers of the plane; the repo's wildcard B (sought imprimitivity but still aimed at finite closure). The principle 'imprimitive UD transfer cell + irrational rotation spectrum implies chi_m(R^2) >= 6 with no closed annulus needed' appears nowhere in the literature I know.

conjectures.name: C2. Lineage primitivity / cocircular imprimitivity threshold
conjectures.statement: (a) For every sectoral slicing of the 12 known chi-5 lineage UDGs (199 to 874 vertices) along their natural rotational symmetries into annular cells, the resulting 5-color transfer digraph is primitive: every recurrent SCC has period 1. (b) Imprimitivity is a cocircular-interface phenomenon: there exists an imprimitive cell (F, alpha) whose interface consists of >= 7 mutually cocircular vertices on a carrier circle of radius rho, with consecutive interface vertices at unit chord (so alpha satisfies 2 rho sin(alpha/2) = 1), and no imprimitive cell exists with interface width 2 or 3.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Pure computation. (a) is refuted by one imprimitive lineage slice (which would be a jackpot for C1, not a loss for the program). (b) is refuted in its positive half if a dense sweep over carrier radii rho in [0.5, 3] and 7 to 12 cocircular interface vertices with interior spindle bracing yields only primitive transfer digraphs; refuted in its negative half by an imprimitive width-2 cell.
conjectures.why_plausible: L57 established that all color forcing in the lineage is adjacency-local; primitivity of transfer is the matrix-level shadow of that fact, so (a) is the expected calibration. For (b): cocircularity is the one Euclidean rigidity (two unit circles meet in <= 2 points; equal chords subtend equal angles) that mass-produces simultaneously constrained wide interfaces, and the L55 monoid obstruction proves width-1 cannot carry phase, suggesting a genuine width threshold.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: (a) could fail because transfer periods were never computed for the lineage (L55/L57 checked pair forcing and port monoids, not annular transfer periods); an overlooked period-2 slice may exist. (b) could fail in either direction: imprimitivity might require far more than 12 cocircular vertices (search horizon too small), or a clever width-2 interface with non-cocircular geometry might already be imprimitive.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The transfer-digraph period of annular slicings of the de Grey/Polymath graphs has never been computed by anyone (this repo checked pair forcing exhaustively, L56/L57, but not cyclic structure of boundary transfer). The cocircular-threshold claim is new; nearest art is Polymath16 ring-coloring discussions, which never considered transfer imprimitivity.

conjectures.name: C3. The Niven split: chi = 5 but chi_B >= 6, with arithmetic censorship as the mechanism
conjectures.statement: chi(R^2) = 5 while chi_B(R^2) >= 6 (indeed no Lebesgue-measurable proper 5-coloring exists), and the split has an arithmetic mechanism: rotationally imprimitive 5-cells exist (C1 Part B true), but for EVERY unit-distance-realizable imprimitive cell the geometry-forced angle alpha has alpha/pi irrational; equivalently, 2 cos alpha is never an algebraic integer whose minimal polynomial divides a Chebyshev-transformed cyclotomic polynomial. Hence no imprimitive annulus ever closes into a finite UDG, no finite chi >= 6 UDG arises from this route (consistent with de Grey's omega <= 3 world and with L56/L57), yet the measurable/Borel obstruction of C1 fires. The answer to Hadwiger-Nelson is definability-relative: 5 for arbitrary colorings, >= 6 for Borel ones.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: (i) For each imprimitive cell found by the sweep, run the sympy test: compute minimal_polynomial(2*cos(alpha)); check whether all conjugates lie in [-2, 2] and the polynomial is of cyclotomic-Chebyshev type (i.e., alpha is a rational multiple of pi). The conjecture predicts NEVER. A single cyclotomic hit refutes the chi = 5 half by closing a finite annulus of N cells with period p not dividing N: a finite UDG with chi >= 6, a happy refutation. (ii) Any explicit Borel 5-coloring of the plane refutes the chi_B half. (iii) Any finite chi >= 6 UDG from another route also refutes the chi = 5 half.
conjectures.why_plausible: L54's backward-from-2050 calibration already flags the definability split as the dark horse. Niven's theorem explains the empirically observed W3 wall (every spindling angle arccos(p/q) in the lineage is an irrational multiple of pi), and the pattern of the program's negative results (abstract clamp exists; 12 of 12 realizable graphs lack it) is exactly what one expects if Euclidean arithmetic censors finite holonomy while leaving measurable holonomy intact.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The program's own front-runner says chi = 6 via a finite UDG; if W3 ever realizes the clamp, the chi = 5 half dies. Or a measurable 5-coloring could exist (refuting C1 Part B and this conjecture simultaneously). Or imprimitive cells might exist at BOTH irrational and rational angles, in which case the split collapses into a plain finite chi >= 6 proof and the truth is uniform chi = 6.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Definability sensitivity for Hadwiger-Nelson has been speculated only along the AC axis (Shelah-Soifer) and as a probability assignment in this repo (L54). A Borel/finite split with a concrete arithmetic mechanism (Niven censorship of annulus closure as the reason the finite object fails to exist while the Borel bound holds) has, to my knowledge, never been stated anywhere.

conjectures.name: C4. Phase clustering (overlap gap) in chains, invisible to pair forcing
conjectures.statement: For every imprimitive cell (F, alpha) with period p, the open chains C_N = union of R^n F, n = 0..N, exhibit an overlap gap: defining the overlap of two proper 5-colorings as the maximum over the 120 color permutations of the fraction of agreeing vertices, there exist 0 < a < b < 1 and N_0 such that for all N >= N_0 no pair of proper 5-colorings has overlap in the open interval (a, b), while same-phase pairs achieve overlap > b. Conversely, the 12 lineage chi-5 graphs have NO overlap gap at any (a, b). Crucially, the gap coexists with L57 pairwise freeness: no single vertex pair is forced, yet the global phase is rigid.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: This is SAT-DECIDABLE, not a sampling claim: couple two copies of the 5-coloring CNF with sequential-counter cardinality constraints forcing the agreement count (after a fixed color permutation, looped over the 120 cosets) into a window [a*M, b*M]. UNSAT across the window certifies the gap; SAT exhibits a mid-overlap pair and refutes it. Runnable today on the 12 lineage graphs (prediction: SAT everywhere, no gap) and on any imprimitive chain the sweep produces (prediction: an UNSAT window appears and widens with N).
conjectures.why_plausible: Imprimitivity literally partitions boundary colorings into p cyclic classes, which forces a sector decomposition of whole-chain colorings; overlap gaps are the standard obstruction to local algorithms, matching the Bernshteyn-style intuition that no-local-rule and no-Borel-coloring travel together. It builds past L57 by moving from pair statistics to solution-space geometry, which the exhaustive pair sweep provably did not measure.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The S_5 alignment may blur sectors: color permutations can map phase sectors onto one another and manufacture mid-range overlaps; or sector interiors may be loose enough (large internal entropy) that overlaps form a continuum despite the phase invariant. Then imprimitivity would be real but invisible to overlap statistics, and the local-algorithm link would need a different observable.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Overlap-gap / clustering analysis has never been applied to unit-distance graph coloring spaces (nearest art: Achlioptas-Coja-Oghlan clustering and Gamarnik's OGP for random CSPs; this repo's L57, which is pair-level only). The claim that UDG 5-coloring spaces can be clustered while every pair is free is new.

experiment.description: Imprimitivity sweep (decides C2, gates C1/C3/C4). New script experiments/axiomatic/shc_transfer_sweep.py. Per cell: (1) build F with sympy-exact coordinates and the rotation R_alpha about c with cos(alpha) and sin(alpha) exact in a quadratic field; for cocircular cells set cos(alpha) = 1 - 1/(2 rho^2) so consecutive carrier points are at unit chord; (2) form F union R(F) and verify ALL unit edges exactly via experiments/_shared/unit_distance_graph.py (no floats); (3) AllSAT-enumerate proper 5-colorings of F union R(F) with cadical plus blocking clauses PROJECTED to the pair (beta restricted to F, pullback of beta restricted to R(F)), yielding the arc set of T(F, alpha, c) in time linear in the number of distinct arcs; (4) networkx strongly connected components; per recurrent SCC compute the period as the gcd of BFS level residues over non-tree arcs; (5) sweep three families: (i) the Moser spindle placed with 2 to 3 vertices cocircular about c (the smallest checkable instance, 7 vertices), (ii) sectors of the smallest lineage graph (199 vertices) sliced along its rotational symmetry (decides C2a), (iii) cells with 6 to 10 cocircular carrier vertices at radius rho swept over a grid of exact values in [0.5, 3], braced internally by spindle edges (attacks C2b). Output per cell cached as JSON (arcs, SCCs, periods) under experiments/axiomatic/_cache/. Decision rule: any recurrent SCC with period >= 2 immediately upgrades to the C1 pipeline (run the C4 coupled-SAT gap check on its chains and the C3 cyclotomic test minimal_polynomial(2*cos(alpha)) via sympy); all-primitive across the sweep falsifies C2b on the family, calibrates C2a, and constrains where imprimitivity can live.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Pure Python, everything already installed per repo facts: python-sat (cadical) for AllSAT with projected blocking clauses on unions of at most ~60 vertices (300 boolean variables, trivial solves); sympy exact rotation fields and the cyclotomic test; networkx for SCC and period; no C compiler needed (no nauty dependency); caching convention matches experiments/**/_cache/. The UnitDistanceGraph interface in experiments/_shared/unit_distance_graph.py provides exact edge verification.
experiment.est_cost: Roughly one day to write and unit-test the script; minutes per cell (projected-arc enumeration typically 10^3 to 10^5 blocking iterations); first full sweep of ~100 cells in 2 to 4 hours on this host. The lineage-slice run (C2a) on the 199-vertex graph is the largest item, est. 1 to 2 hours.

detector_self_check.Q2: Two independent firewalls. First, the conclusion concerns measurable and Borel colorings of a Polish continuum; on the countable set Q^2 every coloring is Borel, so chi_B(Q^2) = chi(Q^2) = 2 and the criterion asserts nothing. Second, the mechanism needs an R_alpha-invariant probability measure supported where the coloring lives: the unique invariant measure on a circle orbit closure is arclength, which charges no countable set, so the eigenfunction step has no support over Q^2 even though Q^2 admits infinite-order rotations (arccos(3/5)). Additionally, the combinatorial input (an imprimitive 5-color cell) cannot exist inside the bipartite Q^2 unit-distance graph. The argument cannot be stated, let alone over-prove, on Q^2.
detector_self_check.Linf: The fuel is an irrational rotation with a compact circular orbit and chord rigidity. The linear isometry group of the L^infty plane is the finite dihedral group D_4: there is NO irrational rotation acting by unit-distance-preserving maps, so the dynamical substrate is absent. The chord structure also degenerates: L^infty circles are squares, unit spheres intersect in segments, and cocircular interfaces collapse into product structure, which produces primitive transfer. The Borel checkerboard 4-coloring of the L^infty plane is untouched, consistent with chi_B = 4 there. This specifically repairs the logged failure mode of the 'borel' world (LEARNINGS: norm-blind Borel/Steinhaus mechanisms over-prove chi >= 7 on L^infty): SHC is norm-aware because its entire engine lives inside SO(2).
detector_self_check.R1: On the line the analogous dynamics is translation by a on (R, Lebesgue), whose unitary spectrum is continuous and contains every unimodular character: psi(x) = exp(2 pi i x/(p a)) solves the phase equation psi(x + a) = exp(2 pi i/p) psi(x), so the holonomy contradiction cannot fire, and the orbit relation is smooth (Borel transversal [0,1)), so transversal-based 2-colorings exist. Correctly, chi_B(R) = chi_m(R) = 2. The argument is rotation-aware by construction: it consumes the compactness of SO(2) orbits and the arithmetic rigidity of irrational rotation point spectra, neither of which exists on R^1.
